footer.tagline: An educational tool for analyzing blood glucose levels using OCR and machine learning technology.
footer.copyright: Blood Glucose Analyzer. For educational purposes only.

disclaimer.title: Medical Disclaimer
disclaimer.body:
This tool is intended for educational and informational purposes only.
It is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
Always consult with a qualified healthcare provider for medical concerns.
disclaimer.inlineShort: For educational purposes only. Not a medical diagnosis.
disclaimer.compactBody: Consult a healthcare provider for medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

hero.subtitle: Upload lab reports, enter values manually, or assess your diabetes risk using AI and ADA-certified guidelines
hero.backendOffline: Backend offline — manual input still works

howItWorks.heading: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three ways to analyze your glucose data — all powered by AI

upload.title: Upload Report
upload.description: Take a photo or upload your lab report — our OCR engine extracts glucose values automatically

manual.title: Enter Manually
manual.description: Type in your glucose values and get instant ADA-based classification results

insights.title: Get Insights
insights.description: Receive detailed classification, recommendations, and diabetes risk prediction

capabilities.heading: Powered by AI
capabilities.subtitle: Industry-leading technologies combined for accurate glucose analysis

ocr.title: OCR Extraction
ocr.description: PaddleOCR with OpenCV preprocessing accurately extracts glucose values from any lab report format

ada.title: ADA Guidelines
ada.description: All classifications follow American Diabetes Association's 2024 Standards of Medical Care

ml.title: ML Risk Prediction
ml.description: Random Forest model trained on PIMA diabetes dataset predicts risk with ~74% accuracy

tests.title: 5 Test Types
tests.description: Supports FBS, HbA1c, PPBS, RBS, and OGTT — all major glucose test formats

supportedTests.heading: Supported Test Types
supportedTests.subtitle: Comprehensive coverage of all major blood glucose tests

cta.heading: Ready to Analyze Your Results?
cta.subtitle: No account needed. All processing is done locally — your data never leaves your device.

hero.subtitle: Upload a lab report, enter values manually, or assess your diabetes risk

upload.description: Upload a lab report image and let AI extract glucose values automatically

manual.label: Manual Input

risk.label: Risk Assessment

fileUpload.heading: Upload your lab report
fileUpload.subheading: Drag and drop your glucose report image here, or click to browse your files
fileUpload.helperText: For best results, ensure the image is clear and glucose values are visible
fileUpload.invalidType: Please upload an image file (PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, or BMP)
fileUpload.tooLarge: File size must be less than 16MB

hints.rbs: Measured at any time of day

riskForm.optionalNote: These factors improve prediction accuracy when provided

ocrEditor.subheader: Review what the AI read from your report. Edit if needed, then confirm.
ocrEditor.lowConfidenceBanner: Some values have low OCR confidence. Compare with your original report before confirming.
ocrEditor.lowConfidenceWarning: Low confidence — verify against your printed report.
ocrEditor.footerNote: Editing a value will re-run ADA classification with your correction.

results.imageTip: Tip: Ensure the image is clear and contains glucose test results

hero.heading: Analysis History
hero.subtitle: Track your glucose trends and review past analyses

noData.heading: No trend data yet
noData.subtitle: Upload reports or enter manual values to see your glucose trends over time

empty.heading: No analyses yet
empty.subtitle: Your analysis history will appear here after you analyze a report or enter values

hero.heading: About Blood Glucose Analyzer
hero.subtitle: An educational platform designed to help individuals understand their blood glucose readings

what.heading: What is this tool?
what.body: The Blood Glucose Analyzer is an educational platform developed as a final-year thesis project. It leverages advanced technologies to help users understand their blood glucose readings:

ocr.title: OCR Technology
ocr.description: PaddleOCR extracts glucose values from lab report images

ada.title: ADA Classification
ada.description: Values are classified according to American Diabetes Association guidelines

ml.title: ML Risk Prediction
ml.description: A Random Forest model predicts diabetes risk based on health factors

ranges.heading: Glucose Reference Ranges
ranges.subtitle: Based on American Diabetes Association (ADA) 2024 guidelines

privacy.heading: Privacy & Data
privacy.body: All analysis is performed locally. Uploaded images are deleted immediately after OCR processing. No data is sent to external servers or stored without your explicit action.

purpose.heading: Educational Purpose
purpose.body: This tool was developed as a thesis project to explore how AI can improve healthcare accessibility in Nepal and similar regions where access to medical professionals may be limited.
